query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "a stylometric application of large language models" computer science
rationale: The candidate paper focuses on fine-tuning strategies for LLM agents using negative examples (failed trajectories), which is a methodological contribution to agent training and data efficiency. This does not address the user's specific question regarding "stylometric application" (analyzing authorship or writing style) or the specific follow-up to the "llmXive" project, as the paper studies a different mechanism (agent tool-use optimization) and a different domain (reasoning tasks vs. stylistic
